title: Add working location search to QissaMap with story pins

requirements.text: Add a visible search input bar to the QissaMap page (QissaMapPage.tsx / QissaMapView.tsx) that allows the user to type a location name and search for it using the Nominatim geocoding API (already used in MapView.tsx).

quotes: make workable search option. Where I enter name of the location

acceptanceCriteria: A search input field is visible on the QissaMap page, positioned prominently (e.g., top-center overlay on the map).
User can type a location name and submit (Enter key or search button).
The Nominatim API is called with the entered query to resolve coordinates.
If no results are found, a user-friendly 'No location found' message is shown.

requirements.text: When a location is found via search, fly/pan the map to that location and place a distinct pin marker at the searched coordinates to visually indicate the searched location.

quotes: it will pin in that location

acceptanceCriteria: The map smoothly pans/zooms to the searched location's coordinates.
A distinct marker (different style from story markers) is placed at the searched location.
The pin remains visible as long as the search result is active.
Clearing the search input removes the location pin.

requirements.text: After searching a location, fetch and display the most popular stories (sorted by likes or pins) within a reasonable radius (e.g., 10–20 km) of the searched coordinates, and render them as floating story cards or labeled markers on the map near the pinned location.

quotes: certain popular story will float in map

acceptanceCriteria: Upon successful location search, the app queries for stories near those coordinates.
Stories are ranked/filtered by popularity (likes or pins count).
Up to 5–10 popular stories are displayed as floating cards or enriched markers on the map near the pinned location.
Each floating story indicator shows the story title and a brief excerpt or category badge.
Clicking a floating story opens the StoryDetailDialog for that story.

constraints: Use the Nominatim API for geocoding (already integrated in MapView.tsx) — do not introduce a new geocoding service.
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
All backend story fetching must go through the existing useQueries hooks.
nonGoals: Replacing or redesigning the existing QissaMap sidebar or clustering behavior.
Adding a new backend search endpoint — use existing proximity/search queries.
Persistent saved searches or search history.
